title: Ceph RADOS Gateway request latency and HTTP 5xx error rate
dataSources: RGW access logs or ops metrics indexed as `ceph:log` / `ceph:pool` with `rgw` dimensions
app: RGW ops log to file + forwarder, or `ceph daemon rgw.* perf dump` scripted input
spl:
index=storage (sourcetype="ceph:log" OR sourcetype="ceph:pool")
| search rgw OR "RADOSGW" OR ":7480" OR ":443"
| eval code=coalesce(http_status, status_code)
| eval lat_ms=coalesce(request_time_ms, latency_ms)
| bin span=5m _time
| stats count as reqs perc95(lat_ms) as p95_ms sum(eval(if(code>=500,1,0))) as err5 by _time, rgw_instance
| eval err_pct=round(err5/reqs*100,2)
| where err_pct > 1 OR p95_ms > 500
description: S3/Swift workloads depend on predictable RGW latency. Rising p95 with elevated 5xx rates often tracks backend OSD saturation or Keystone integration failures.
value: Protects application SLAs for cloud-native apps using Ceph object storage.
implementation: Prefer structured ops logs with `http_status` at index time. If only unstructured, use `rex` for Apache/nginx-style lines.
